old_claim.note: Herbalism was treated as an enabling capability for iatrochemistry without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct herbalism dependency remains because Paracelsian iatrochemistry is scoped to chemical and mineral medical explanations, often challenging Galenic herbal practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Overview and history sections: the article frames iatrochemistry as chemical medicine rooted in alchemy and associated with Paracelsus, not as an herbalism dependency.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports iatrochemistry as a chemically framed medical movement connected to alchemy and Paracelsus.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Herbalism is the Galenic and botanical medical context the node contrasts with; retaining it direct would invert that relationship.

ontology_before: Iatrochemistry depended directly on Herbalism, implying plant-based medicine was a causal prerequisite for Paracelsian chemical medicine.
ontology_after: Iatrochemistry keeps Alchemy and Scientific Method as direct prerequisites; herbalism remains adjacent medical context outside the direct graph edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: herbalism is no longer a direct prerequisite for iatrochemistry_paracelsus.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to herbal pharmacopoeias rather than chemical medicine.
A stronger source showed herbalism directly enabled Paracelsian iatrochemistry rather than serving as a rival tradition.
A dependent required iatrochemistry_paracelsus to mean continuity from plant-based Galenic practice.
short_reason: Herbalism is medical context, not the chemical-medicine prerequisite.
